title: Oldenlandia bojeri
intro: Oldenlandia bojeri, also known as Bojer's Oldenlandia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia bojeri include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Oldenlandia bojeri is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia bojeri is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Oldenlandia, which is a group of small to medium-sized annual or perennial herbs. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 species. The classification of Oldenlandia bojeri is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
